summary: Tune the WAF layer: add a managed rule group in count mode, retune the rate limit, add or remove an IP-set CIDR, remove a rule, or reorder rule priority — each guardrailed with a second approver. Flipping a rule to active block mode stays engineer-only: it can reject live traffic immediately.

operations.title: Add an AWS managed rule group (count mode)
operations.summary: Add an AWS managed rule group in watch-only mode, so it observes traffic without blocking anything yet.
operations.description: Attach an additional AWS-vendored managed rule group to the Web ACL at a new evaluation priority, with override_action set to count so it observes traffic and cannot block it. The safest way to add coverage before deciding whether to enforce it.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The existing WAFv2 Web ACL to extend, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed ACL can be targeted; the new managed rule group is attached in count mode, so it observes traffic but cannot block it.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: New rule name
params.help: Identifies the new rule within the Web ACL; later ops (priority, action, deletion) select an existing rule by this name.

params.name: priority
params.label: Priority
params.help: Evaluation order — lower runs first. Must not collide with an existing rule's priority.

params.name: managed_rule_group_name
params.label: Managed rule group
params.help: Only AWS-published groups not already attached to this ACL are offered.

params.name: vendor_name
params.label: Vendor name
params.help: Always "AWS" — this op only attaches AWS-vendored managed rule groups.

params.name: metric_name
params.label: CloudWatch metric name
params.help: CloudWatch metric name for this rule's visibility config; required (no automatic default — there is no templating engine to derive AWS-<group name> at request time).

params.name: cloudwatch_metrics_enabled
params.label: CloudWatch metrics enabled
params.help: Always enabled so the new rule's traffic is observable in CloudWatch.

params.name: sampled_requests_enabled
params.label: Sampled requests enabled
params.help: Always enabled so request samples are available for this rule in CloudWatch.

operations.title: Swap a rule group's override action (count / none)
operations.summary: Turn a rule group's blocking on or off. Turning it on can reject live traffic immediately, so an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Replace whichever empty override_action child (count/none) the selected rule or managed rule group holds with the requested one. The rule is chosen by the rule_name selector; the desired choice comes from the override_action discriminator's segments map. Refuses CHOICE_NOT_EMPTY if the existing child carries config (e.g. custom_request_handling), CHOICE_AMBIGUOUS if the rule's override_action block is malformed. Moving to NONE lets the rule's own block/allow decision take effect and can reject live application traffic immediately, so this is authored and applied by an engineer.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The existing WAFv2 Web ACL containing the rule whose override_action is changing, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed ACL can be targeted; because a switch to NONE can let the rule's own block decision reject live application traffic immediately, this operation is engineer-only.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule to change
params.help: Name of the existing rule or managed rule group, exactly as it appears in the ACL. Selects which rule's override_action block is swapped.

params.name: override_action
params.label: New override action
params.help: COUNT observes only. NONE lets the rule's own block/allow decision take effect and can reject real traffic. Replaces whichever empty override_action child (count/none) the rule currently holds.

operations.title: Retune the rate-based rule threshold
operations.summary: Raise or lower the request threshold that triggers the rate limit, without touching any other rule.
operations.description: Adjust the request-per-5-minute limit on the existing rate-based rule that protects the fronted web application. Raising it reduces false-positive blocks under load spikes; lowering it tightens abuse protection. In-place update, no rule reordering.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The existing WAFv2 Web ACL containing the rate-based rule to retune, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed ACL can be targeted; only the numeric request threshold changes — no rules are added, removed, or reordered.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rate-based rule
params.help: Name of the existing rate-based rule to retune.

params.name: new_rate_limit
params.label: New rate limit
params.help: Requests per 5-minute window per aggregation key (usually source IP) before the rule engages.

operations.title: Add a CIDR to an IP set
operations.summary: Add one IP address or range to an allow or block list. Only that list's membership changes.
operations.description: Append one CIDR to an existing IP set's address list (a partner egress allowlist, office allowlist, or abuse blocklist). Does not change which rule references the set, only its membership.

params.name: ip_set
params.label: IP set
params.help: The existing WAFv2 IP set to append the CIDR to, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed set can be targeted; only the set's address membership changes, not which ACL rules reference it.

params.name: new_cidr
params.label: CIDR to add

bounds.semantic: no 0.0.0.0/0; reject IPv4 prefixes shorter than /16 unless engineer override

params.help: e.g. 203.0.113.12/32 — a single partner egress IP or a tight range.

operations.title: Remove a CIDR from an IP set
operations.summary: Remove one IP or range from a list. This can cut off that source's access, or un-block it, depending on how the list is used.
operations.description: Remove one CIDR from an existing IP set's address list. If the set backs an allow rule, removing an entry withdraws that source's access; if it backs a blocklist, removing an entry un-blocks that source.

params.name: ip_set
params.label: IP set
params.help: The existing WAFv2 IP set to remove the CIDR from, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed set can be targeted; removing an entry withdraws that source's access if the set backs an allow rule, or un-blocks it if the set backs a blocklist.

params.name: cidr_to_remove
params.label: CIDR to remove
params.help: Must exactly match an existing entry in the set.

operations.title: Remove a rule from the Web ACL
operations.summary: Remove one rule from the firewall's rule list. If it was a protection rule, this lowers your security coverage.
operations.description: Decommission a single rule block — a managed rule group, IP-set reference, or rate-based rule — from the ACL. In-place update; the ACL itself is untouched. Removing a managed protection group lowers the estate's security posture.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The existing WAFv2 Web ACL to remove a rule from, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed ACL can be targeted; the ACL itself stays in place and only the named rule block is decommissioned, in place and reversibly.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule to remove
params.help: Name of the rule as it appears in the ACL.

params.name: confirm_rule_name
params.label: Confirm rule name
params.help: Type the rule name again to confirm removal.

operations.title: Reorder a rule's evaluation priority
operations.summary: Move a rule earlier or later in the evaluation order; no rule is added or removed.
operations.description: Relocate an existing rule to a different evaluation position by changing its priority (WAF evaluates rules low-to-high). Common when an allow rule needs to run ahead of, or a noisy rule needs to run behind, the managed groups. In-place update, no destroy.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The existing WAFv2 Web ACL whose rule is being reordered, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed ACL can be targeted; only a rule's priority number changes (WAF evaluates low-to-high), with no rule added or removed.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule to move
params.help: Name of the rule whose priority changes.

params.name: new_priority
params.label: New priority
params.help: Must not collide with another rule's priority; the codemod shows the resulting evaluation order for review.

operations.title: Swap the Web ACL default action (allow / block)
operations.summary: Set the firewall's default to allow or block unmatched traffic. Blocking can reject live traffic immediately, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Replace whichever empty default_action child (allow/block) the ACL holds with the requested one — a singleton target, no rule selector needed. Refuses CHOICE_NOT_EMPTY if the existing child carries config, CHOICE_AMBIGUOUS if default_action is malformed. This is the estate-wide fallback decision for the whole Web ACL, so an engineer authors and reviews it; switching to BLOCK can immediately reject live application traffic that previously fell through to allow.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: default_action
params.label: New default action
params.help: ALLOW passes unmatched requests; BLOCK rejects them. Replaces whichever empty default_action child (allow/block) the ACL currently holds.

operations.title: Change the Web ACL scope (CLOUDFRONT / REGIONAL)
operations.summary: Switch the firewall between global and regional scope. This REPLACES it: new identity, and protection briefly drops while it's rebuilt.
operations.description: Change the Web ACL scope between CLOUDFRONT and REGIONAL. This argument is ForceNew: Terraform destroys and recreates the ACL, momentarily removing protection from every associated resource, and CLOUDFRONT additionally requires the provider region us-east-1. Engineer-authored and reviewed; not reversible without a second replacement.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: new_scope
params.label: New scope
params.help: CLOUDFRONT (requires provider region us-east-1) or REGIONAL. Changing scope recreates the ACL.

operations.title: Swap a rule's own action block (allow / block / captcha / challenge / count)
operations.summary: Change what a rule does on a match: allow, block, count, or challenge. Blocking can reject live requests right away; an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Replace whichever empty action child (allow/block/captcha/challenge/count) the selected rule holds with the requested one: the rule's OWN statement action, distinct from waf-change-rule-group-action, which swaps override_action on managed/rule-group rules. The rule is chosen by the rule_name selector; the desired choice comes from the action discriminator's segments map. Refuses CHOICE_NOT_EMPTY if the existing child carries config, CHOICE_AMBIGUOUS if the rule's action block is malformed. Moving a rule to BLOCK (or off COUNT) can reject live requests immediately, so an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule to change
params.help: Name of the existing rule that uses its own action{} block (not override_action) whose action changes. Selects which rule's action block is swapped.

params.name: action
params.label: New action
params.help: Replaces whichever empty action child (allow/block/captcha/challenge/count) the rule currently holds.

operations.title: Change a rate-based rule's aggregation key type
operations.summary: Change how the rate limit groups requests together, such as by IP or a custom key. This changes who gets rate-limited and when.
operations.description: Set rate_based_statement.aggregate_key_type on an existing rate-based rule — one of IP, FORWARDED_IP, CONSTANT, or CUSTOM_KEYS — which changes how request counts are aggregated and therefore the rule's effective rate-limiting behavior. FORWARDED_IP also needs forwarded_ip_config, CUSTOM_KEYS a custom_key, and CONSTANT a scope_down_statement, so an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rate-based rule
params.help: Name of the existing rate-based rule to retune.

params.name: new_aggregate_key_type
params.label: New aggregate key type
params.help: How request counts are aggregated; default IP.

operations.title: Change a rate-based rule's evaluation window
operations.summary: Change the time window the rate limit uses to count requests. A shorter window reacts faster; a longer one tolerates bursts better.
operations.description: Set rate_based_statement.evaluation_window_sec on an existing rate-based rule — the lookback window in seconds used to count requests — to one of 60, 120, 300, or 600. A shorter or longer window retunes the rule's effective sensitivity, so an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rate-based rule
params.help: Name of the existing rate-based rule to retune.

params.name: new_evaluation_window_sec
params.label: New evaluation window (seconds)
params.help: Lookback window in seconds; one of 60, 120, 300, 600 (default 300).

operations.title: Change a protected resource's request-body inspection size limit
operations.summary: Change the largest request size the firewall inspects for a resource. A smaller limit lets large malicious payloads slip through unchecked.
operations.description: Set association_config.request_body.<resource_type>.default_size_inspection_limit — the maximum request-body size AWS WAF inspects for a given protected-resource type — to KB_16, KB_32, KB_48, or KB_64. The limit is written inside the selected resource type's request_body sub-block; an ACL that declares no association_config yet is refused rather than guessed at. Shrinking the limit lets payloads beyond the cutoff bypass rule inspection (a security regression across every association it configures), so an engineer authors and reviews it.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: protected_resource_type
params.label: Protected resource type
params.help: Which request_body sub-block to configure.

params.name: default_size_inspection_limit
params.label: Body inspection size limit
params.help: Maximum request-body size AWS WAF inspects for the selected resource type.

operations.title: Enable or disable CloudWatch metrics for the Web ACL
operations.summary: Turn CloudWatch metrics for the firewall on or off. Monitoring only — it doesn't change which traffic is allowed or blocked.
operations.description: Toggle the ACL-level visibility_config.cloudwatch_metrics_enabled flag, which controls whether the Web ACL emits metrics to CloudWatch. Observability-only change; it does not alter any rule's match statement or block/allow decision. Guardrailed.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: cloudwatch_metrics_enabled
params.label: CloudWatch metrics enabled
params.help: true emits ACL metrics to CloudWatch; false disables them.

operations.title: Enable or disable request sampling for the Web ACL
operations.summary: Turn on or off sampling of matching requests for review in the console. Monitoring only — no effect on allow or block decisions.
operations.description: Toggle the ACL-level visibility_config.sampled_requests_enabled flag, which controls whether AWS WAF stores a sample of matching requests for review in the console. Observability-only change; it does not alter any rule's match statement or block/allow decision. Guardrailed.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: sampled_requests_enabled
params.label: Sampled requests enabled
params.help: true stores a sample of matching requests for console review; false disables sampling.

operations.title: Rename the Web ACL CloudWatch metric
operations.summary: Rename the firewall's CloudWatch metric label. Cosmetic only — no effect on which traffic is allowed or blocked.
operations.description: Set the ACL-level visibility_config.metric_name — the friendly CloudWatch metric name for the Web ACL. Cosmetic label with no effect on rule matching or block/allow decisions. Must be 1-128 characters of A-Z, a-z, 0-9, hyphen or underscore with no whitespace, and cannot be a name reserved by AWS WAF (All, Default_Action).

params.name: web_acl
params.label: Web ACL
params.help: The web ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: metric_name
params.label: CloudWatch metric name
params.help: 1-128 chars of A-Z, a-z, 0-9, hyphen, underscore; no whitespace; cannot be 'All' or 'Default_Action'.

operations.title: Change an IP set's scope (CloudFront / Regional)
operations.summary: Move an IP list between global and regional scope. This REPLACES it — new identity, and it briefly drops out of every rule using it.
operations.description: Change an existing IP set's `scope` between CLOUDFRONT and REGIONAL. This is a ForceNew argument: Terraform destroys and recreates the aws_wafv2_ip_set, changing its ARN and momentarily removing it from every rule that references it, and CLOUDFRONT scope requires the provider region to be us-east-1. Engineer-authored and reviewed.

params.name: ip_set
params.label: IP set
params.help: The IP set to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: new_scope
params.label: New scope
params.help: CLOUDFRONT (requires the provider region to be us-east-1) or REGIONAL. Changing scope replaces the IP set and breaks references to its old ARN.

operations.title: Change an IP set's IP address version (IPv4 / IPv6)
operations.summary: Switch an IP list between IPv4 and IPv6. This REPLACES it — new identity, and matching briefly drops out of every rule using it.
operations.description: Change an existing IP set's `ip_address_version` between IPV4 and IPV6. This is a ForceNew argument: Terraform destroys and recreates the aws_wafv2_ip_set, changing its ARN and momentarily removing IP-based matching from every rule that references it, and all `addresses` must be re-expressed in the chosen version. Engineer-authored and reviewed.

params.name: ip_set
params.label: IP set
params.help: The IP set to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: new_ip_address_version
params.label: New IP address version
params.help: IPV4 or IPV6. Changing the version replaces the IP set and requires all addresses to match the new version.

operations.title: Provision a Web ACL
operations.summary: Create a WAFv2 Web ACL shell with a bounded default action and metrics on. Protection rules are added one at a time.
operations.description: Create an aws_wafv2_web_acl with a bounded scope and default action and its visibility metrics on. The protection rules are NOT authored here: they are added ONE at a time as reviewed follow-up, so this provisions the shell an engineer completes. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: acl_name
params.label: Web ACL name
params.help: The name of the Web ACL (written to the name attribute).

params.name: scope
params.label: Scope
params.help: REGIONAL protects regional resources (ALB, API Gateway); CLOUDFRONT protects a CloudFront distribution.

params.name: default_action
params.label: Default action
params.help: What happens to a request no rule matches. The engineer expands this into its default_action block form at review.

params.name: visibility_metric_name
params.label: Metric name
params.help: The CloudWatch metric name for this Web ACL.

params.name: cloudwatch_metrics_enabled
params.label: Cloudwatch metrics enabled
params.help: Emit CloudWatch metrics for the Web ACL; written into the draft's visibility_config.

params.name: sampled_requests_enabled
params.label: Sampled requests enabled
params.help: Store sampled requests for the Web ACL; written into the draft's visibility_config.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: The default action (allow or block) sets what happens to every request no rule matches; confirm it, and expand it into its default_action block form during review.
This provisions the Web ACL shell with its metrics on. The protection rules are added ONE at a time as reviewed follow-up, never bulk self-service.

operations.title: Provision an IP set
operations.summary: Create a WAFv2 IP set of CIDRs. An IP set feeds allow or deny rules, so a wrong or broad range shifts access.
operations.description: Create an aws_wafv2_ip_set: a named list of CIDRs a Web ACL rule can allow or deny. The scope and IP version are bounded; the addresses are confirmed at review because they directly widen or narrow what a rule matches. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: ip_set_name
params.label: IP set name
params.help: The name of the IP set (written to the name attribute).

params.name: scope
params.label: Scope
params.help: REGIONAL for regional resources; CLOUDFRONT for a CloudFront distribution.

params.name: ip_address_version
params.label: IP version
params.help: Whether the addresses are IPv4 or IPv6 CIDRs.

params.name: addresses
params.label: Addresses (CIDR)
params.help: The CIDR ranges in the set, for example 203.0.113.0/24. Each range widens or narrows what a rule using this set matches.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: Confirm each CIDR is correct and intended: an IP set feeds allow or deny rules, so a wrong or overly-broad range silently widens or blocks access.
